mechanism_narrative: A 6-month growth acceleration in real GDP signals an expansion in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of accelerating real GDP growth are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in industrial production signals a contraction in supply capacity that drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of declining industrial production are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of rising unemployment are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month increase in housing starts signals an expansion in residential investment that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of rising housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the federal funds rate signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of rising federal funds rate are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 1-month decline in initial jobless claims signals a loosening labor market that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of declining initial jobless claims are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in real personal spending signals an expansion in aggregate demand that drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of rising real personal spending are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in the consumer price index signals a loosening monetary cycle that expands real cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of declining consumer price index are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
